context: =  pain in groin / thigh after hip arthroplasty Prevalence of pain:   40%; ~ 120,000 hip arthroplasties per year in USA Incidence:   1.7% (within first 2 weeks) Cx:   wound dehiscence, infection Incidence:   50-60% (within 8 weeks after surgery) Risk factors:   male gender, DISH, history of heterotopic ossification, osteoarthritis with preexisting heterotopic bone, ankylosing spondylitis Incidence:   in up to 18% Predisposition:   osteoporosis, osteolysis, stress shielding (= bone resorption due to decreased stress to bone) typically at base of greater trochanter and calcar head, failure of ab
